findings.title: Critical Accessibility Gaps for Visually Impaired Users
findings.description: Visually impaired users are unable to navigate the course catalog or complete the enrollment flow due to the lack of ARIA roles and keyboard support. This significantly hinders their ability to access essential features of the platform.
assumptions: Visually impaired users rely on screen readers to navigate online platforms.

evidence.excerpt: Screen reader cannot navigate course catalog or enrollment flow.
Quiz forms use custom radio buttons and checkboxes that are invisible to screen readers.
James uses JAWS screen reader and is enrolled in mandatory corporate training through LearnPath.

recommendations: Implement ARIA roles and keyboard navigation for all interactive elements.

findings.title: Lack of Captions and Transcripts in Video Player
findings.description: The absence of captions, transcripts, and keyboard controls in the video player blocks access for deaf, hard-of-hearing, and motor-impaired users, violating multiple WCAG standards.
assumptions: Accessible video content is essential for inclusivity in online learning.

evidence.excerpt: Video player lacks captions, transcripts, and keyboard controls.
James... the video player is completely unusable with a keyboard.

recommendations: Add closed captions and transcripts to all video content.

findings.title: Color Contrast Failures Across UI Components
findings.description: 60% of UI components fail to meet WCAG AA contrast ratios, which can lead to accessibility issues for users with visual impairments.
assumptions: Proper color contrast is necessary for readability and accessibility.

evidence.excerpt: Accessibility audit found that 60% of text elements fail WCAG AA contrast ratios.

recommendations: Revise color schemes to ensure compliance with WCAG AA contrast ratios.

findings.title: Inaccessible Quiz and Assessment Forms
findings.description: Quiz forms are not accessible to assistive technologies, making it impossible for users with disabilities to participate in assessments.
assumptions: Accessible assessments are critical for fair evaluation of all students.

evidence.excerpt: Quiz forms use custom radio buttons and checkboxes that are invisible to screen readers.

recommendations: Ensure all quiz components are compatible with assistive technologies.

findings.title: Need for VPAT to Meet Compliance Requirements
findings.description: Prospective clients in government and education sectors require a VPAT to document compliance with WCAG 2.1 AA standards before making purchasing decisions.
assumptions: A VPAT is essential for securing contracts with educational and governmental institutions.

evidence.excerpt: Government and education sector prospects require a Voluntary Product Accessibility Template (VPAT).
Emily's agency needs a VPAT before any purchasing decision.

recommendations: Conduct a full accessibility audit and create a VPAT.

summary: The analysis reveals critical accessibility gaps in LearnPath that significantly hinder users with disabilities, particularly visually impaired and motor-impaired individuals. Key areas of concern include navigation issues, lack of accessible video content, color contrast failures, and the need for a VPAT to meet compliance requirements. Immediate action is recommended to address these issues to enhance usability and secure potential contracts.
